message.reasoning_content:
We are in stage lib-003-chain. We need to create a third library that depends on the second library, preserving the full call chain.

Existing generated files:
- library-chain/core/core.c
- library-chain/core/core.h
- library-chain/core/main.c
- library-chain/core/Makefile
- library-chain/feature/feature.c
- library-chain/feature/feature.h
- library-chain/feature/main.c
- library-chain/feature/Makefile

We need to create a third library (let's call it chain) that depends on the feature library (which in turn depends on core). We must preserve the full call chain: so the third library should call a function from the second library, which calls a function from the first.

We are expected to create: library-chain/chain (project)

Validators that will run:
- pipeline-references-feature: failure_class=api_contract
- chain-sample-runs: failure_class=runtime_behavior

Thus, we need to create a new library (chain) that uses the feature library. We'll create:
- chain.h: header for the chain library (declares a public function that uses feature's feature_add)
- chain.c: implementation of the chain library (includes feature.h and calls feature_add)
- main.c: a sample caller that uses the chain library (includes chain.h and calls the chain function)
- Makefile: to build the chain library and the sample, linking with the feature and core libraries.

We must not modify existing files (unless necessary, but we should only add new files for the chain project).

We'll follow the same pattern as the feature library.

Directory: library-chain/chain/

Files:
- chain.h
- chain.c
- main.c
- Makefile

chain.h:
#ifndef CHAIN_H
#define CHAIN_H

int chain_add(int a, int b);

#endif

chain.c:
#include "chain.h"
#include "../feature/feature.h"   // or we can include feature.h from the feature directory

int chain_add(int a, int b) {
    // We call the feature_add function from the feature library.
    return feature_add(a, b);
}

main.c:
#include <stdio.h>
#include "chain.h"

int main() {
    int result = chain_add(2, 3);
    printf("%d\n", result);
    return 0;
}

Makefile:
We need to build:
- libcore.a (from core)
- libfeature.a (from feature)
- libchain.a (from chain)
- then link main.o with libchain.a, libfeature.a, libcore.a? Actually, note: chain.c calls feature_add, which in turn calls add. So if we link main with libchain.a, we must also link with libfeature.a and libcore.a because chain.c depends on feature, and feature depends on core.

Alternatively, we can build the chain library as a static library that only contains chain.o, and then link with the other two.

We can design the Makefile similarly to the feature's Makefile but now we have two dependencies: feature and core.

We can either build the dependencies by recursing into their directories or by compiling the source files directly.

We'll do:

CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
FEATURE_DIR = ../feature
CORE_DIR = ../core

all: sample

sample: main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o sample main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a

main.o: main.c chain.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c main.c

libchain.a: chain.o
	ar rcs libchain.a chain.o

chain.o: chain.c chain.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c chain.c

libfeature.a: $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c -o feature.o
	ar rcs libfeature.a feature.o
	rm -f feature.o

libcore.a: $(CORE_DIR)/core.c $(CORE_DIR)/core.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(CORE_DIR)/core.c -o core.o
	ar rcs libcore.a core.o
	rm -f core.o

clean:
	rm -f *.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a sample

We must use tabs for the commands.

Now, we need to output JSON with the new files (only the chain directory). We do not repeat existing files.

Let's craft the content strings, escaping appropriately.

chain.h:
#ifndef CHAIN_H
#define CHAIN_H

int chain_add(int a, int b);

#endif

Content: "#ifndef CHAIN_H\\n#define CHAIN_H\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b);\\n\\n#endif\\n"

chain.c:
#include "chain.h"
#include "../feature/feature.h"

int chain_add(int a, int b) {
    return feature_add(a, b);
}

Content: 
'#include "chain.h"\\n#include "../feature/feature.h"\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b) {\\n    return feature_add(a, b);\\n}\\n'

Note: we need to escape the quotes in the includes: #include \\"chain.h\\" and #include \\"../feature/feature.h\\"

So: 
'#include \\"chain.h\\"\\n#include \\"../feature/feature.h\\"\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b) {\\n    return feature_add(a, b);\\n}\\n'

main.c:
#include <stdio.h>
#include "chain.h"

int main() {
    int result = chain_add(2, 3);
    printf("%d\\n", result);
    return 0;
}

Content:
'#include <stdio.h>\\n#include \\"chain.h\\"\\n\\nint main() {\\n    int result = chain_add(2, 3);\\n    printf(\\\"%d\\\\n\\\", result);\\n    return 0;\\n}\\n'

Makefile:
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
FEATURE_DIR = ../feature
CORE_DIR = ../core

all: sample

sample: main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o sample main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a

main.o: main.c chain.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c main.c

libchain.a: chain.o
	ar rcs libchain.a chain.o

chain.o: chain.c chain.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c chain.c

libfeature.a: $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c -o feature.o
	ar rcs libfeature.a feature.o
	rm -f feature.o

libcore.a: $(CORE_DIR)/core.c $(CORE_DIR)/core.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(CORE_DIR)/core.c -o core.o
	ar rcs libcore.a core.o
	rm -f core.o

clean:
	rm -f *.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a sample

We need to escape tabs as \\t and also escape the $ in $(CC) etc. but $ is not special in JSON, so we can leave it.

We'll write the Makefile content as a string with escaped newlines and tabs.

Let's write line by line:

"CC = gcc\\nCFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -std=c11\\nFEATURE_DIR = ../feature\\nCORE_DIR = ../core\\n\\nall: sample\\n\\nsample: main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o sample main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a\\n\\nmain.o: main.c chain.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c main.c\\n\\nlibchain.a: chain.o\\n\\tar rcs libchain.a chain.o\\n\\nchain.o: chain.c chain.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c chain.c\\n\\nlibfeature.a: $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c -o feature.o\\n\\tar rcs libfeature.a feature.o\\n\\trm -f feature.o\\n\\nlibcore.a: $(CORE_DIR)/core.c $(CORE_DIR)/core.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(CORE_DIR)/core.c -o core.o\\n\\tar rcs libcore.a core.o\\n\\trm -f core.o\\n\\nclean:\\n\\trm -f *.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a sample\\n"

Now, we output the JSON.

We also need a notes field.

Let's create the JSON.

Note: We must not include any extra text.

We'll output:

{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "library-chain/chain/chain.h",
      "content": "#ifndef CHAIN_H\\n#define CHAIN_H\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b);\\n\\nendif\\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/chain/chain.c",
      "content": "#include \\"chain.h\\"\\n#include \\"../feature/feature.h\\"\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b) {\\n    return feature_add(a, b);\\n}\\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/chain/main.c",
      "content": "#include <stdio.h>\\n#include \\"chain.h\\"\\n\\nint main() {\\n    int result = chain_add(2, 3);\\n    printf(\\\"%d\\\\n\\\", result);\\n    return 0;\\n}\\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/chain/Makefile",
      "content": "CC = gcc\\nCFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -std=c11\\nFEATURE_DIR = ../feature\\nCORE_DIR = ../core\\n\\nall: sample\\n\\nsample: main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o sample main.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a\\n\\nmain.o: main.c chain.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c main.c\\n\\nlibchain.a: chain.o\\n\\tar rcs libchain.a chain.o\\n\\nchain.o: chain.c chain.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c chain.c\\n\\nlibfeature.a: $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(FEATURE_DIR)/feature.c -o feature.o\\n\\tar rcs libfeature.a feature.o\\n\\trm -f feature.o\\n\\nlibcore.a: $(CORE_DIR)/core.c $(CORE_DIR)/core.h\\n\\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $(CORE_DIR)/core.c -o core.o\\n\\tar rcs libcore.a core.o\\n\\trm -f core.o\\n\\nclean:\\n\\trm -f *.o libchain.a libfeature.a libcore.a sample\\n"
    }
  ],
  "notes": "Created chain library that depends on feature and calls its feature_add function, preserving the full call chain, with a sample caller and Makefile."
}

Make sure to fix the chain.h content: we had an extra backslash before endif? Actually, we wrote: "#ifndef CHAIN_H\\n#define CHAIN_H\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b);\\n\\nendif\\n" but it should be "#endif\\n". Let's correct: "#ifndef CHAIN_H\\n#define CHAIN_H\\n\\nint chain_add(int a, int b);\\n\\n#endif\\n"

Now, output only the JSON.
